steps.description: environment setup: uv sync --frozen || uv sync

steps.command: uv run test "$(sy --version)" = "Symphony $(python -c 'import importlib.metadata as m; print(m.version("symphony"))')"

steps.description: scoped tests for changed test files: tests/test_cli.py

notes: selective execution: narrowed scoped tests to keywords [version].

criterionResults.reason: Ran `uv run sy --version`: exit 0 and output contains "Symphony".

criterionResults.criterion: Version value is read from the package metadata (`importlib.metadata`) — not hardcoded.
criterionResults.reason: Agent-constructed check did not pass (Ran `uv run test "$(sy --version)" = "Symphony $(python -c 'import importlib.metadata as m; print(m.version("symphony"))')"`: exited 1 (expected 0).) — this corroborating probe is not authoritative, so the criterion could not be verified by execution and needs manual review.

evidence.summary: output of `uv run test "$(sy --version)" = "Symphony $(python -c 'import importlib.metadata as m; print(m.version("symphony"))')"`

summary: Delivery verdict: manual_review_required. 2 criteria (1 pass, 1 not_evaluable).
ticketQualityIssues: AC-2 mixes an observable outcome (printed version matches package metadata) with an implementation directive ('read via importlib.metadata', 'not hardcoded'). The directive itself is not black-box observable; the criterion is verifiable only as the proxy that output equals the metadata version. A reviewer could not distinguish a correctly-implemented dynamic read from a value that happens to match if hardcoded at the current version.
